questions.prompt: When PSE syncs a user from Microsoft Entra ID, which Entra ID attribute does PSE use as the matching key to associate the user with a PBX extension?
choices: The user's email address (the mail attribute)
questions.explanation: PSE uses userPrincipalName as the matching key. This survives an admin changing the user's primary email in Entra ID; if the UPN changes (rare, like a domain rebrand), the link breaks and the user has to be re-associated.

questions.prompt: A user's PSE extension has two passwords: a User Password (for Linkus login) and a Registration Password (for SIP endpoint registration). These can be set to different values.
questions.explanation: They are deliberately separate fields. A leaked user password shouldn't give a fraudster SIP registration. When a user reports 'Linkus logs in but my desk phone says auth failed', check the Registration Password, not the User Password.

questions.prompt: Which of the following are valid reasons to choose bulk CSV import over M365 user sync for creating extensions? (Select all that apply.)
choices: The customer's identity lives in a Google Workspace tenant, not M365
The customer wants extensions for users in a different tenant that won't migrate for several months
The customer wants ongoing automatic sync of identity changes
The customer prefers their user records to be PBX-local instead of identity-provider-owned
The CSV import gives faster setup than M365 sync for the initial cutover
questions.explanation: M365 sync is the right call when identity lives in M365 and the customer wants ongoing sync. Bulk CSV makes sense for non-M365 identity sources, parallel tenants, or customers who prefer PBX-local records. CSV is not faster for the initial cutover (M365 sync is essentially one-click); the speed argument is wrong.
